freedomConfigAdaptation.description: Tenant-safe governance and rendering values that can change without altering schema validation, tenant isolation, version immutability, cycle detection, or store-before-emit ordering.

keys.semantic: How long a breaking schema change may wait for operator approval before expiring.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune approval latency while preserving explicit approval for breaking changes.

keys.semantic: Fallback decision when an approval item has no explicit operator decision.
keys.rationale: DEFER is the safest default; tenant adaptation may choose stricter or faster governance.

keys.semantic: Time between a deprecation action and archive eligibility.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants can lengthen or shorten migration windows without deleting schema history.

keys.semantic: Cache lifetime for rendered DAG visualization responses.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may trade freshness for lower rendering cost while the graph source remains tenant-scoped.

grammarAdaptation.description: FLOW-11 has no tenant-editable schema grammar surface. Tenants may tune governance windows and labels without changing schema validation semantics.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: Platform admin, platform support, and tenant admin role boundaries are fixed for certification. Tenant adaptation may not make the full platform DAG visible to tenant-user roles.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Tenant schema-governance policies that can change while fixed registry, compatibility, DAG, and publication invariants remain intact.

edgeCases.name: Short approval window
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can require breaking schema changes to be reviewed quickly.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T202 reads the tenant-scoped window before writing approval expiry.

edgeCases.name: Defer by default
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can keep unresolved breaking changes pending rather than approving automatically.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T202 falls back to DEFER when no explicit approval decision is supplied.

edgeCases.name: Long deprecation window
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can give downstream consumers a longer migration window before archive.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T200 computes archive eligibility from the tenant-scoped TTL.

edgeCases.name: Fresh DAG rendering
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can reduce DAG cache lifetime for active schema migration periods.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T208 reads a tenant-scoped cache TTL before serving rendered topology.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenants cannot change without breaking portability or authorization certification.
rules: T189 obtains tenant scope from AsyncLocalStorage, not caller-provided tenant fields.
T189 stores accepted schema records before emitting downstream schema events.
T191 uses three-state DFS with WHITE, GRAY, and BLACK traversal states.
T193 classifies compatibility without bypassing version validation.
T194 uses optimistic concurrency for schema version immutability.
T194 publishes only after inline validation and compatibility checks pass.
T200 never deletes schema history as part of deprecation.
T202 never approves breaking changes without an explicit configured or operator decision.
All FLOW-11 services remain FLOW_SCOPED and return DataProcessResult.

tenant-a-strict-schema-governance.description: Tenant A shortens approval windows and keeps unresolved breaking changes deferred.
tenant-a-strict-schema-governance.impact: Breaking changes require timely operator review while additive changes continue through the normal validation path.
tenant-a-strict-schema-governance.testable: Tenant A adaptation evidence shows the policy in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory and fork CI still passes.

tenant-a-active-migration-mode.description: Tenant A lowers DAG cache TTL during a schema migration window.
tenant-a-active-migration-mode.impact: Operators see fresher topology renders while schema storage, compatibility, and tenant scope remain fixed.
tenant-a-active-migration-mode.testable: Tenant A before/after assertion shows the adapted TTL in FREEDOM defaults without changing MACHINE invariants.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: Task type IDs T189 through T208

passCriteria: Tenant A can apply a governance-window or visualization-cache policy change without altering MACHINE constraints.
The adapted policy is present in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory.
At least one before/after behavior assertion proves the adapted policy changes approval or visualization behavior.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing token, 401 for cross-tenant token, and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
